questions.q: Which neurotransmitter system is most commonly targeted by antidepressant medications?
questions.explanation: SSRIs increase serotonin by blocking reuptake, while other antidepressants target norepinephrine or both. GABA-targeting drugs are anxiolytics, not primarily antidepressants.

questions.q: What is the primary function of dopamine in the brain?
questions.explanation: Dopamine is central to reward processing, motivation, and voluntary motor control. Dysfunction in dopamine systems is associated with Parkinson's disease and depression.

questions.q: Which neurotransmitter is the main inhibitory transmitter in the spinal cord and brain?
questions.explanation: GABA is the primary inhibitory neurotransmitter in the brain and spinal cord, reducing neuronal excitability. Glutamate is the main excitatory transmitter.

questions.q: What is the primary excitatory neurotransmitter in the mammalian nervous system?
questions.explanation: Glutamate is the main excitatory neurotransmitter, acting through NMDA, AMPA, and kainate receptors. Excessive glutamate can cause excitotoxicity.

questions.q: Which neurotransmitter is released at the neuromuscular junction?
questions.explanation: Acetylcholine is the neurotransmitter at the neuromuscular junction, binding nicotinic receptors and opening ion channels. Blocking acetylcholine causes paralysis.
Serotonin regulates mood, circadian rhythms, sleep, appetite, and temperature. Dopamine, not serotonin, is central to motor planning.

questions.q: Which neurotransmitter is most involved in reward and addiction?
questions.explanation: Dopamine is the key reward neurotransmitter; drugs of abuse increase dopamine in the nucleus accumbens and ventral tegmental area, driving addiction.

questions.q: What is the reuptake process in synaptic transmission?
options: Active transport of neurotransmitters back into the presynaptic neuron to recycle or degrade them
Passive diffusion of neurotransmitters across the synapse
Binding of neurotransmitters to postsynaptic receptors
questions.explanation: Reuptake removes neurotransmitters from the synaptic cleft via transporter proteins, terminating synaptic transmission. Many drugs, including SSRIs, block reuptake to increase neurotransmitter availability.

questions.q: Which neurotransmitter is synthesized from the amino acid tyrosine?
questions.explanation: Dopamine is synthesized from tyrosine via L-DOPA. Serotonin is made from tryptophan, and GABA from glutamate.

questions.q: What do benzodiazepines do at the synaptic level?
options: They enhance GABA-mediated inhibition by increasing chloride conductance
questions.explanation: Benzodiazepines allosterically enhance GABA_A receptor function, increasing chloride influx and neuronal inhibition. This produces anxiolytic and sedative effects.
